content.content:
1	# Story 2.5: Docs-only deepwiki path
2	
3	Status: ready-for-dev
4	
5	<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
6	
7	## Story
8	
9	As a developer who wants a skill for a documentation site (no repo),
10	I want to run `deepwiki <doc-url>` with just a documentation URL,
11	so that I get a wiki skill built entirely from documentation content.
12	
13	## Acceptance Criteria
14	
15	1. **Given** a documentation URL (e.g., `https://docs.example.com`) without a repo URL, **When** `deepwiki <doc-url>` is invoked, **Then** the doc detection chain runs against the provided URL as the primary source, **And** a wiki skill is created with `source_type: docs-only` in the brief.
16	
17	2. **Given** a docs-only invocation, **When** BS generates the brief, **Then** `source_type` is set to `docs-only`, **And** the brief does not require a repo URL, **And** the brief references the documentation URL as the primary source.
18	
19	3. **Given** a docs-only invocation where the URL is unreachable, **When** the pipeline starts, **Then** the pipeline halts with a clear error: "Documentation URL unreachable: <url>", **And** exit code reflects the error.
20	
21	4. **Given** a standard repo URL (e.g., `https://github.com/org/repo`), **When** `deepwiki` is invoked with that URL, **Then** the standard repo-based deepwiki flow runs (not the docs-only path).
22	
23	## Tasks / Subtasks
24	
25	- [ ] Task 1: Add docs-only URL detection and short-circuit to step-auto-scope.md (AC: #1, #3, #4)
26	  - [ ] 1.1 Insert new §0 "URL Type Detection" before §1 (Load Context). Read the target URL/path from the pipeline context. Apply the heuristic: GitHub repo URL (`github.com/{owner}/{repo}` pattern) or local filesystem path (`/`, `./`, `~`) → repo mode (continue to §1). Anything else starting with `https://` or `http://` → docs-only mode (continue to §0a).
27	  - [ ] 1.2 Create §0a "Docs-Only Short-Circuit": validate the URL is reachable via HEAD check with 5-second timeout (`curl -sI --max-time 5 {url}`). On 2xx/3xx: proceed. On 4xx/5xx, DNS failure, or timeout: HARD HALT with error message "Documentation URL unreachable: {url} — {status or error}" and exit code 2 (`input-invalid`). Emit error envelope per §10 format.
28	  - [ ] 1.3 In §0a, after URL validation passes: write a minimal brief via the canonical writer with `source_type: docs-only`, `doc_urls: [{url: target_url, label: "Primary Documentation"}]`, `scope_type: docs-only`, `source_authority: community`, `source_repo` set to the doc URL for reference. Use the doc site domain as the default `skill_name` (e.g., `docs-example-com` from `https://docs.example.com`). Set `version` to `1.0.0` (no package manifest available).
29	  - [ ] 1.4 In §0a, write the analysis report with `source_type: docs-only` in frontmatter, `confirmed_units` containing one entry with `shape: docs-only`, `confidence: 1.0`, `export_count: 0`, `package_count: 0`.
30	  - [ ] 1.5 In §0a, emit the success envelope: `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success","report_path":"...","brief_paths":["..."],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":1,"skipped":0,"maybe":0},"exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto","source_type":"docs-only"}`. The `source_type` field signals downstream consumers (BS) to skip repo-based enrichment.
31	  - [ ] 1.6 In §0a, chain to `{nextStepFile}` (health-check.md) — skip §1 through §11 entirely.
32	
33	- [ ] Task 2: Add docs-only conditional to step-auto-brief.md (AC: #1, #2)
34	  - [ ] 2.1 At §1 "Load Upstream Brief", after extracting optional fields, add a check: if `source_type` is `docs-only` in the parsed brief, skip §2 (Run Doc Detection) and §3 (Enrich Brief with Detected Docs) — the doc URL is already in the brief's `doc_urls`.
35	  - [ ] 2.2 When docs-only is detected, log: "Docs-only brief — skipping repo-based doc detection. Doc URLs provided by upstream." Proceed directly to §4 (Validate & Write Enriched Brief).
36	  - [ ] 2.3 Ensure all brief fields remain intact through the skip — `source_type`, `source_authority`, `doc_urls`, `scope_type` must pass through unmodified to the canonical writer.
37	
38	- [ ] Task 3: Update AN SKILL.md workflow documentation (AC: #1, #4)
39	  - [ ] 3.1 In the Stages table, add a note: "Step 1a includes §0 docs-only URL detection — doc URLs short-circuit auto-scope entirely"
40	  - [ ] 3.2 In the Auto mode path description, add: "When the target is a documentation URL (not a GitHub repo or local path), auto-scope detects the docs-only input at §0, validates URL reachability, writes a docs-only brief, and emits the envelope without performing source analysis."
41	  - [ ] 3.3 In the Invocation Contract, note that `--project-path` can accept a documentation URL for docs-only mode. Document that the URL type heuristic determines the mode.
42	
43	- [ ] Task 4: Verify backward compatibility (AC: #4)
44	  - [ ] 4.1 Verify that GitHub repo URLs (https://github.com/org/repo, https://github.com/org/repo.git) continue to route to the standard auto-scope flow (§1→§2→§3→...)
45	  - [ ] 4.2 Verify that local filesystem paths (/path/to/project, ./relative/path) continue to route to the standard auto-scope flow
46	  - [ ] 4.3 Verify that the interactive AN path (no `[auto]`) is entirely unchanged — §0 is only executed when `{auto_mode}` is true
47	  - [ ] 4.4 Verify that the `forge`, `forge-quick`, `onboard`, and `maintain` pipeline aliases are unaffected
48	
49	- [ ] Task 5: Run validation (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
50	  - [ ] 5.1 Verify §0/§0a in step-auto-scope.md is clearly structured and the docs-only short-circuit path is unambiguous
51	  - [ ] 5.2 Verify the docs-only conditional in step-auto-brief.md cleanly skips §2 and §3 without altering §4-§6
52	  - [ ] 5.3 Verify the envelope `source_type: "docs-only"` field does not break the envelope schema (it is a new field — existing consumers ignore unknown fields)
53	  - [ ] 5.4 Run `npm test` — full regression suite passes (no regressions)
54	
55	## Dev Notes
56	
57	### Architecture: Docs-Only as a Short-Circuit in Auto-Scope
58	
59	Story 2.5 adds a docs-only URL detection at the very beginning of step-auto-scope.md (§0) — before any source analysis happens. The change is a **conditional short-circuit**: doc URLs are detected and handled entirely within §0/§0a, then the flow chains directly to health-check. The entire existing auto-scope flow (§1→§11) is untouched for repo URLs.
60	
61	**Current flow (Story 2.4):**
62	```
63	§1 Context → §2 Manifest Scan → §3 Shape Detect → §3a Threshold Check → §4/§4a Scope → ... → §11 Chain
64	```
65	
66	**New flow (Story 2.5):**
67	```
68	§0 URL Type Detection
69	  ├── doc URL → §0a Docs-Only Short-Circuit → health-check (skip §1-§11)
70	  └── repo URL or local path → §1 → §2 → §3 → §3a → ... → §11 (unchanged)
71	```
72	
73	The short-circuit produces the same output artifacts as the full flow (analysis report + brief + envelope) but with docs-only values.
74	
75	[Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#FR→File Mapping — "FR-20 Docs-only path maps to src/skf-analyze-source/ + src/skf-brief-skill/"]
76	
77	### URL Type Detection Heuristic
78	
79	The detection at §0 uses a simple, deterministic heuristic:
80	
81	| URL Pattern | Classification | Route |
82	|------------|---------------|-------|
83	| `github.com/{owner}/{repo}` | GitHub repo | §1 (standard auto-scope) |
84	| Starts with `/`, `./`, `~/`, or `~` | Local filesystem path | §1 (standard auto-scope) |
85	| `gitlab.com/...`, `bitbucket.org/...`, etc. | Git hosting (future) | §1 (standard auto-scope) |
86	| Any other `https://` or `http://` URL | Documentation URL | §0a (docs-only) |
87	
88	This heuristic is intentionally simple. Edge cases (e.g., a GitHub Pages URL like `https://org.github.io/repo`) are classified as doc URLs, which is the correct behavior — the user wants a skill from that documentation site, not from the underlying repo.
89	
90	**Fallback safety:** If the heuristic misclassifies a repo URL as a doc URL, the user will get a docs-only skill. This is recoverable — they can re-run with the correct GitHub URL. The reverse (doc URL classified as repo) cannot happen because only explicit `github.com` patterns route to repo mode.
91	
92	### Docs-Only Brief Generation in AN
93	
94	When §0a generates the docs-only brief, it uses the canonical brief writer (`skf-write-skill-brief.py`), same as the standard flow. The brief fields:
95	
96	```yaml
97	name: docs-example-com            # derived from URL domain (kebab-case)
98	version: "1.0.0"                  # no manifest — default
99	source_repo: "https://docs.example.com"  # doc URL for reference
100	source_type: docs-only
101	source_authority: community       # forced for docs-only (T3 content)
102	language: ""                      # unknown from docs alone — CS will detect
103	forge_tier: forge                 # default tier
104	scope:
105	  type: docs-only
106	  include: []                     # no source patterns
107	  exclude: []
108	  notes: "Docs-only skill created from documentation URL"
109	  rationale: "User-provided documentation URL — no source code analysis"
110	doc_urls:
111	  - url: "https://docs.example.com"
112	    label: "Primary Documentation"
113	```
114	
115	The brief passes schema validation because `source_type: docs-only` makes `source_repo` optional and `doc_urls` required (both satisfied). `skf-write-skill-brief.py` already handles `docs-only` in its `VALID_SOURCE_TYPES` set.
116	
117	[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/assets/skill-brief-schema.md — "When source_type: docs-only: source_repo optional, doc_urls required"]
118	[Source: src/shared/scripts/skf-write-skill-brief.py:149 — "VALID_SOURCE_TYPES = {'source', 'docs-only'}"]
119	
120	### Downstream Pipeline: Already Docs-Only Aware
121	
122	The docs-only path through the deepwiki pipeline (`AN[auto] BS[auto] CS TS[min:90] EX`) works because every downstream workflow already handles `source_type: docs-only`:
123	
124	| Workflow | Docs-Only Handling | Status |
125	|----------|-------------------|--------|
126	| **AN** (step-auto-scope) | §0a short-circuit — writes brief + envelope, skips source analysis | **This story** |
127	| **BS** (step-auto-brief) | Conditional skip of §2 (doc detection) and §3 (enrichment) — brief passes through | **This story** |
128	| **BS** (gather-intent §3.2) | Full interactive docs-only branch already implemented | Ready |
129	| **BS** (headless-args) | Accepts `source_type=docs-only` and `doc_urls` arguments | Ready |
130	| **CS** (extract.md) | Skips source file extraction, sets `extraction_mode: "docs-only"`, proceeds to doc-fetcher | Ready |
131	| **CS** (sub/fetch-docs.md) | Full doc fetching with subpage discovery, parallel fetch, T3 citations | Ready |
132	| **TS** (scoring-rules) | Adjusted scoring: removes Signature Accuracy & Type Coverage, redistributes weights | Ready |
133	| **EX** | Standard export — no source_type-specific behavior | Ready |
134	
135	The only gaps are in AN (detection + short-circuit) and BS (conditional skip) — both addressed by this story.
136	
137	[Source: src/skf-create-skill/references/extract.md — "Skip source file extraction when source_type: docs-only"]
138	[Source: src/skf-create-skill/references/sub/fetch-docs.md — "primary extraction source in Scenario D (docs-only, SaaS, closed-source)"]
139	[Source: src/skf-test-skill/references/scoring-rules.md — "Signature Accuracy & Type Coverage removed when docsOnly: true"]
140	
141	### BS Step-Auto-Brief Conditional
142	
143	The docs-only conditional in step-auto-brief.md is minimal:
144	
145	```
146	§1 Load Upstream Brief → extract source_type
147	  ├── source_type == "docs-only" → skip §2, skip §3 → §4 (write brief as-is)
148	  └── source_type != "docs-only" → §2 (doc detection) → §3 (enrichment) → §4
149	```
150	
151	When docs-only, BS does NOT re-derive any fields from the brief. The brief from AN already has the correct `source_type`, `doc_urls`, `scope_type`, and `source_authority`. BS's role is to validate it through the canonical writer and chain to step-auto-validate.md.
152	
153	The doc detection script (`skf-detect-docs.py`) requires `--repo-url` which expects a GitHub API-compatible URL. Calling it with a plain docs URL would fail. The conditional skip prevents this error.
154	
155	[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/step-auto-brief.md:72 — "uv run python {detectDocsScript} --repo-url {source_repo}"]
156	
157	### Envelope Extension
158	
159	The success envelope from §0a adds one new field: `source_type: "docs-only"`. This is backward-compatible — existing consumers ignore unknown fields in the envelope JSON. The field serves as a signal for BS to skip doc detection.
160	
161	The error envelope on unreachable URL uses the existing format unchanged (`halt_reason: "input-invalid"`, `exit_code: 2`).
162	
163	[Source: src/skf-analyze-source/references/step-auto-scope.md:62 — "existing error envelope format"]
164	
165	### Project Structure Notes
166	
167	- No new files created — this story modifies existing step files in `src/skf-analyze-source/references/` and `src/skf-brief-skill/references/`
168	- No new scripts or Python files — URL detection is a step-file-level heuristic (string pattern matching), not deterministic computation
169	- No new test files — step files are validated structurally via `npm test`, not with pytest
170	- The URL type heuristic is intentionally simple (5 pattern checks) — it belongs in the step file, not in a Python script
171	
172	### Previous Story Intelligence
173	
174	**Story 2.4 patterns to replicate:**
175	
176	1. **Conditional branching preserves backward compatibility.** Story 2.4 inserted §3a between §3 and §4 without changing the single-scope path. Story 2.5 follows the same principle: §0 is a conditional short-circuit before §1 — the entire repo-based flow (§1→§11) is completely untouched.
177	
178	2. **Envelope extension, not creation.** Story 2.4 kept the same envelope schema with N > 1 values. Story 2.5 adds one new field (`source_type`) to the existing envelope schema — backward-compatible.
179	
180	3. **Analysis report frontmatter uses confirmed_units array.** Story 2.5 writes one entry with `shape: docs-only` — the array structure is already in place.
181	
182	[Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/2-4-auto-decomposition-for-massive-repos.md#Dev Notes]
183	
184	**Story 2.1 patterns to replicate:**
185	
186	1. **Shape→scope mapping.** Story 2.1 established the mapping table. Docs-only doesn't need mapping — it's a direct assignment of `scope_type: docs-only`.
187	
188	2. **Brief writing through canonical writer.** All briefs go through `skf-write-skill-brief.py`. Docs-only briefs are no exception.
189	
190	[Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/2-4-auto-decomposition-for-massive-repos.md#Previous Story Intelligence]
191	
192	**Story 2.3 patterns to replicate:**
193	
194	1. **Headless gate convention.** Step-auto-validate.md auto-approves in headless mode. Docs-only briefs follow the same path through step-auto-validate.
195	
196	[Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/2-4-auto-decomposition-for-massive-repos.md#Previous Story Intelligence]
197	
198	### Git Intelligence
199	
200	Recent commits on `deepwiki-zero-ceremony-skill-creation` branch:
201	- `46ddc84` feat(story-2.4): Auto-decomposition for massive repos
202	- `50968ee` feat(story-2.3): Auto-brief validation with progressive fallback
203	- `06652e9` feat(story-2.2): Auto-brief generation for BS
204	- `9d1adc5` feat(story-2.1): Auto-scope mode for AN
205	
206	Stories 2.1→2.2→2.3→2.4 committed sequentially. Story 2.5 continues this pattern.
207	
208	### Git Workflow
209	
210	- **Branch**: `deepwiki-zero-ceremony-skill-creation` (epic-level branch for all Epic 2 stories)
211	- **Commit onto**: This branch, next sequential commit after story 2.4
212	- **PR target**: `main` (single PR when Epic 2 is complete)
213	
214	### Cross-Platform Requirements
215	
216	Per project convention (PRs #311/#316/#366/#368/#387):
217	- Use `encoding="utf-8"` when reading/writing files in any Python code
218	- Use `Path.as_posix()` for paths in JSON output
219	- Windows CI (`windows-latest`) is the gatekeeper — Linux is silent on encoding issues
220	
221	### Dependencies
222	
223	| Dependency | Story | Status | What This Story Needs |
224	|-----------|-------|--------|----------------------|
225	| `step-auto-scope.md` | 2.1, 2.4 | done | The file being modified — docs-only detection inserted before §1 |
226	| `step-auto-brief.md` | 2.2 | done | The file being modified — docs-only conditional skip for §2/§3 |
227	| `skf-detect-docs.py` | 1.2 | done | NOT invoked for docs-only (user provides URL directly), but context for understanding what BS skips |
228	| `skf-write-skill-brief.py` | pre-existing | done | Canonical brief writer — already handles `source_type: docs-only` |
229	| `step-auto-validate.md` | 2.3 | done | Downstream consumer — validates and presents the brief for approval (informational, not modified) |
230	
231	No blocking dependencies. All prerequisite step files and shared modules are complete.
232	
233	### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
234	
235	1. **Do NOT invoke `skf-detect-docs.py` for docs-only URLs.** The script requires a GitHub repo URL for API calls. Passing a plain docs URL would fail or produce misleading results. The user-provided URL IS the doc source — no detection needed.
236	
237	2. **Do NOT skip AN entirely for docs-only.** The pipeline alias `AN[auto] BS[auto] CS TS[min:90] EX` always starts with AN. AN must handle docs-only gracefully (short-circuit) rather than expecting the forger to skip it — that would require pipeline-level conditional logic that doesn't exist.
238	
239	3. **Do NOT modify BS's interactive flow (gather-intent.md §3.2).** The existing interactive docs-only branch is complete and working. This story only modifies the `[auto]` mode path (step-auto-brief.md).
240	
241	4. **Do NOT modify the forger's pipeline mode parsing.** The deepwiki alias expansion (`AN[auto] BS[auto] CS TS[min:90] EX`) is the same for both repo and docs-only URLs. URL type detection happens inside AN, not at the forger level.
242	
243	5. **Do NOT create a separate pipeline alias for docs-only.** The epics specify a single `deepwiki` command that handles both repo URLs and doc URLs. The URL type heuristic in AN determines the path.
244	
245	6. **Do NOT store doc content in the brief or analysis report.** Only the URL is stored. Content fetching is CS's responsibility (sub/fetch-docs.md handles it).
246	
247	7. **Do NOT modify downstream workflows (CS, TS, EX).** They already handle `source_type: docs-only` correctly. This story's scope is AN detection + BS conditional skip.
248	
249	### Files to Modify
250	
251	| File | Change |
252	|------|--------|
253	| `src/skf-analyze-source/references/step-auto-scope.md` | Insert §0 (URL Type Detection) and §0a (Docs-Only Short-Circuit) before §1; update §0→§1 routing |
254	| `src/skf-brief-skill/references/step-auto-brief.md` | Add conditional at §1: when `source_type == "docs-only"`, skip §2 and §3, proceed to §4 |
255	| `src/skf-analyze-source/SKILL.md` | Update auto mode path description and invocation contract to document docs-only URL detection |
256	
257	### Files to Read (Not Modify)
258	
259	| File | Purpose |
260	|------|---------|
261	| `src/skf-forger/SKILL.md` | Pipeline mode parsing — understand alias expansion and data flow between AN→BS |
262	| `src/skf-brief-skill/references/gather-intent.md` | §3.2 docs-only branch — reference for existing pattern (validation, source_authority forcing) |
263	| `src/skf-brief-skill/references/headless-args.md` | Headless arg contract — confirms `source_type=docs-only` and `doc_urls` are valid headless args |

265	| `src/shared/scripts/skf-write-skill-brief.py` | Brief writer — confirms `VALID_SOURCE_TYPES` includes `docs-only` |

267	| `src/skf-create-skill/references/extract.md` | CS docs-only handling — verify downstream is ready |
268	| `src/skf-create-skill/references/sub/fetch-docs.md` | Doc fetching — verify CS can fetch from user-provided doc URLs |

270	| `src/shared/references/pipeline-contracts.md` | Pipeline data flow — understand handoff between AN and BS |

# Story 2.5: Docs-only deepwiki path

Status: ready-for-dev

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a developer who wants a skill for a documentation site (no repo),
I want to run `deepwiki <doc-url>` with just a documentation URL,
so that I get a wiki skill built entirely from documentation content.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** a documentation URL (e.g., `https://docs.example.com`) without a repo URL, **When** `deepwiki <doc-url>` is invoked, **Then** the doc detection chain runs against the provided URL as the primary source, **And** a wiki skill is created with `source_type: docs-only` in the brief.

2. **Given** a docs-only invocation, **When** BS generates the brief, **Then** `source_type` is set to `docs-only`, **And** the brief does not require a repo URL, **And** the brief references the documentation URL as the primary source.

3. **Given** a docs-only invocation where the URL is unreachable, **When** the pipeline starts, **Then** the pipeline halts with a clear error: "Documentation URL unreachable: <url>", **And** exit code reflects the error.

4. **Given** a standard repo URL (e.g., `https://github.com/org/repo`), **When** `deepwiki` is invoked with that URL, **Then** the standard repo-based deepwiki flow runs (not the docs-only path).

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] Task 1: Add docs-only URL detection and short-circuit to step-auto-scope.md (AC: #1, #3, #4)
  - [ ] 1.1 Insert new §0 "URL Type Detection" before §1 (Load Context). Read the target URL/path from the pipeline context. Apply the heuristic: GitHub repo URL (`github.com/{owner}/{repo}` pattern) or local filesystem path (`/`, `./`, `~`) → repo mode (continue to §1). Anything else starting with `https://` or `http://` → docs-only mode (continue to §0a).
  - [ ] 1.2 Create §0a "Docs-Only Short-Circuit": validate the URL is reachable via HEAD check with 5-second timeout (`curl -sI --max-time 5 {url}`). On 2xx/3xx: proceed. On 4xx/5xx, DNS failure, or timeout: HARD HALT with error message "Documentation URL unreachable: {url} — {status or error}" and exit code 2 (`input-invalid`). Emit error envelope per §10 format.
  - [ ] 1.3 In §0a, after URL validation passes: write a minimal brief via the canonical writer with `source_type: docs-only`, `doc_urls: [{url: target_url, label: "Primary Documentation"}]`, `scope_type: docs-only`, `source_authority: community`, `source_repo` set to the doc URL for reference. Use the doc site domain as the default `skill_name` (e.g., `docs-example-com` from `https://docs.example.com`). Set `version` to `1.0.0` (no package manifest available).
  - [ ] 1.4 In §0a, write the analysis report with `source_type: docs-only` in frontmatter, `confirmed_units` containing one entry with `shape: docs-only`, `confidence: 1.0`, `export_count: 0`, `package_count: 0`.
  - [ ] 1.5 In §0a, emit the success envelope: `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success","report_path":"...","brief_paths":["..."],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":1,"skipped":0,"maybe":0},"exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto","source_type":"docs-only"}`. The `source_type` field signals downstream consumers (BS) to skip repo-based enrichment.
  - [ ] 1.6 In §0a, chain to `{nextStepFile}` (health-check.md) — skip §1 through §11 entirely.

- [ ] Task 2: Add docs-only conditional to step-auto-brief.md (AC: #1, #2)
  - [ ] 2.1 At §1 "Load Upstream Brief", after extracting optional fields, add a check: if `source_type` is `docs-only` in the parsed brief, skip §2 (Run Doc Detection) and §3 (Enrich Brief with Detected Docs) — the doc URL is already in the brief's `doc_urls`.
  - [ ] 2.2 When docs-only is detected, log: "Docs-only brief — skipping repo-based doc detection. Doc URLs provided by upstream." Proceed directly to §4 (Validate & Write Enriched Brief).
  - [ ] 2.3 Ensure all brief fields remain intact through the skip — `source_type`, `source_authority`, `doc_urls`, `scope_type` must pass through unmodified to the canonical writer.

- [ ] Task 3: Update AN SKILL.md workflow documentation (AC: #1, #4)
  - [ ] 3.1 In the Stages table, add a note: "Step 1a includes §0 docs-only URL detection — doc URLs short-circuit auto-scope entirely"
  - [ ] 3.2 In the Auto mode path description, add: "When the target is a documentation URL (not a GitHub repo or local path), auto-scope detects the docs-only input at §0, validates URL reachability, writes a docs-only brief, and emits the envelope without performing source analysis."
  - [ ] 3.3 In the Invocation Contract, note that `--project-path` can accept a documentation URL for docs-only mode. Document that the URL type heuristic determines the mode.

- [ ] Task 4: Verify backward compatibility (AC: #4)
  - [ ] 4.1 Verify that GitHub repo URLs (https://github.com/org/repo, https://github.com/org/repo.git) continue to route to the standard auto-scope flow (§1→§2→§3→...)
  - [ ] 4.2 Verify that local filesystem paths (/path/to/project, ./relative/path) continue to route to the standard auto-scope flow
  - [ ] 4.3 Verify that the interactive AN path (no `[auto]`) is entirely unchanged — §0 is only executed when `{auto_mode}` is true
  - [ ] 4.4 Verify that the `forge`, `forge-quick`, `onboard`, and `maintain` pipeline aliases are unaffected

- [ ] Task 5: Run validation (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [ ] 5.1 Verify §0/§0a in step-auto-scope.md is clearly structured and the docs-only short-circuit path is unambiguous
  - [ ] 5.2 Verify the docs-only conditional in step-auto-brief.md cleanly skips §2 and §3 without altering §4-§6
  - [ ] 5.3 Verify the envelope `source_type: "docs-only"` field does not break the envelope schema (it is a new field — existing consumers ignore unknown fields)
  - [ ] 5.4 Run `npm test` — full regression suite passes (no regressions)

## Dev Notes

### Architecture: Docs-Only as a Short-Circuit in Auto-Scope

Story 2.5 adds a docs-only URL detection at the very beginning of step-auto-scope.md (§0) — before any source analysis happens. The change is a **conditional short-circuit**: doc URLs are detected and handled entirely within §0/§0a, then the flow chains directly to health-check. The entire existing auto-scope flow (§1→§11) is untouched for repo URLs.

**Current flow (Story 2.4):**
```
§1 Context → §2 Manifest Scan → §3 Shape Detect → §3a Threshold Check → §4/§4a Scope → ... → §11 Chain
```

**New flow (Story 2.5):**
```
§0 URL Type Detection
  ├── doc URL → §0a Docs-Only Short-Circuit → health-check (skip §1-§11)
  └── repo URL or local path → §1 → §2 → §3 → §3a → ... → §11 (unchanged)
```

The short-circuit produces the same output artifacts as the full flow (analysis report + brief + envelope) but with docs-only values.

[Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#FR→File Mapping — "FR-20 Docs-only path maps to src/skf-analyze-source/ + src/skf-brief-skill/"]

### URL Type Detection Heuristic

The detection at §0 uses a simple, deterministic heuristic:

| URL Pattern | Classification | Route |
|------------|---------------|-------|
| `github.com/{owner}/{repo}` | GitHub repo | §1 (standard auto-scope) |
| Starts with `/`, `./`, `~/`, or `~` | Local filesystem path | §1 (standard auto-scope) |
| `gitlab.com/...`, `bitbucket.org/...`, etc. | Git hosting (future) | §1 (standard auto-scope) |
| Any other `https://` or `http://` URL | Documentation URL | §0a (docs-only) |

This heuristic is intentionally simple. Edge cases (e.g., a GitHub Pages URL like `https://org.github.io/repo`) are classified as doc URLs, which is the correct behavior — the user wants a skill from that documentation site, not from the underlying repo.

**Fallback safety:** If the heuristic misclassifies a repo URL as a doc URL, the user will get a docs-only skill. This is recoverable — they can re-run with the correct GitHub URL. The reverse (doc URL classified as repo) cannot happen because only explicit `github.com` patterns route to repo mode.

### Docs-Only Brief Generation in AN

When §0a generates the docs-only brief, it uses the canonical brief writer (`skf-write-skill-brief.py`), same as the standard flow. The brief fields:

```yaml
name: docs-example-com            # derived from URL domain (kebab-case)
version: "1.0.0"                  # no manifest — default
source_repo: "https://docs.example.com"  # doc URL for reference
source_type: docs-only
source_authority: community       # forced for docs-only (T3 content)
language: ""                      # unknown from docs alone — CS will detect
forge_tier: forge                 # default tier
scope:
  type: docs-only
  include: []                     # no source patterns
  exclude: []
  notes: "Docs-only skill created from documentation URL"
  rationale: "User-provided documentation URL — no source code analysis"
doc_urls:
  - url: "https://docs.example.com"
    label: "Primary Documentation"
```

The brief passes schema validation because `source_type: docs-only` makes `source_repo` optional and `doc_urls` required (both satisfied). `skf-write-skill-brief.py` already handles `docs-only` in its `VALID_SOURCE_TYPES` set.

[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/assets/skill-brief-schema.md — "When source_type: docs-only: source_repo optional, doc_urls required"]
[Source: src/shared/scripts/skf-write-skill-brief.py:149 — "VALID_SOURCE_TYPES = {'source', 'docs-only'}"]

### Downstream Pipeline: Already Docs-Only Aware

The docs-only path through the deepwiki pipeline (`AN[auto] BS[auto] CS TS[min:90] EX`) works because every downstream workflow already handles `source_type: docs-only`:

| Workflow | Docs-Only Handling | Status |
|----------|-------------------|--------|
| **AN** (step-auto-scope) | §0a short-circuit — writes brief + envelope, skips source analysis | **This story** |
| **BS** (step-auto-brief) | Conditional skip of §2 (doc detection) and §3 (enrichment) — brief passes through | **This story** |
| **BS** (gather-intent §3.2) | Full interactive docs-only branch already implemented | Ready |
| **BS** (headless-args) | Accepts `source_type=docs-only` and `doc_urls` arguments | Ready |
| **CS** (extract.md) | Skips source file extraction, sets `extraction_mode: "docs-only"`, proceeds to doc-fetcher | Ready |
| **CS** (sub/fetch-docs.md) | Full doc fetching with subpage discovery, parallel fetch, T3 citations | Ready |
| **TS** (scoring-rules) | Adjusted scoring: removes Signature Accuracy & Type Coverage, redistributes weights | Ready |
| **EX** | Standard export — no source_type-specific behavior | Ready |

The only gaps are in AN (detection + short-circuit) and BS (conditional skip) — both addressed by this story.

[Source: src/skf-create-skill/references/extract.md — "Skip source file extraction when source_type: docs-only"]
[Source: src/skf-create-skill/references/sub/fetch-docs.md — "primary extraction source in Scenario D (docs-only, SaaS, closed-source)"]
[Source: src/skf-test-skill/references/scoring-rules.md — "Signature Accuracy & Type Coverage removed when docsOnly: true"]

### BS Step-Auto-Brief Conditional

The docs-only conditional in step-auto-brief.md is minimal:

```
§1 Load Upstream Brief → extract source_type
  ├── source_type == "docs-only" → skip §2, skip §3 → §4 (write brief as-is)
  └── source_type != "docs-only" → §2 (doc detection) → §3 (enrichment) → §4
```

When docs-only, BS does NOT re-derive any fields from the brief. The brief from AN already has the correct `source_type`, `doc_urls`, `scope_type`, and `source_authority`. BS's role is to validate it through the canonical writer and chain to step-auto-validate.md.

The doc detection script (`skf-detect-docs.py`) requires `--repo-url` which expects a GitHub API-compatible URL. Calling it with a plain docs URL would fail. The conditional skip prevents this error.

[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/step-auto-brief.md:72 — "uv run python {detectDocsScript} --repo-url {source_repo}"]

### Envelope Extension

The success envelope from §0a adds one new field: `source_type: "docs-only"`. This is backward-compatible — existing consumers ignore unknown fields in the envelope JSON. The field serves as a signal for BS to skip doc detection.

The error envelope on unreachable URL uses the existing format unchanged (`halt_reason: "input-invalid"`, `exit_code: 2`).

[Source: src/skf-analyze-source/references/step-auto-scope.md:62 — "existing error envelope format"]

### Project Structure Notes

- No new files created — this story modifies existing step files in `src/skf-analyze-source/references/` and `src/skf-brief-skill/references/`
- No new scripts or Python files — URL detection is a step-file-level heuristic (string pattern matching), not deterministic computation
- No new test files — step files are validated structurally via `npm test`, not with pytest
- The URL type heuristic is intentionally simple (5 pattern checks) — it belongs in the step file, not in a Python script

### Previous Story Intelligence

**Story 2.4 patterns to replicate:**

1. **Conditional branching preserves backward compatibility.** Story 2.4 inserted §3a between §3 and §4 without changing the single-scope path. Story 2.5 follows the same principle: §0 is a conditional short-circuit before §1 — the entire repo-based flow (§1→§11) is completely untouched.

2. **Envelope extension, not creation.** Story 2.4 kept the same envelope schema with N > 1 values. Story 2.5 adds one new field (`source_type`) to the existing envelope schema — backward-compatible.

3. **Analysis report frontmatter uses confirmed_units array.** Story 2.5 writes one entry with `shape: docs-only` — the array structure is already in place.

[Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/2-4-auto-decomposition-for-massive-repos.md#Dev Notes]

**Story 2.1 patterns to replicate:**

1. **Shape→scope mapping.** Story 2.1 established the mapping table. Docs-only doesn't need mapping — it's a direct assignment of `scope_type: docs-only`.

2. **Brief writing through canonical writer.** All briefs go through `skf-write-skill-brief.py`. Docs-only briefs are no exception.

[Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/2-4-auto-decomposition-for-massive-repos.md#Previous Story Intelligence]

**Story 2.3 patterns to replicate:**

1. **Headless gate convention.** Step-auto-validate.md auto-approves in headless mode. Docs-only briefs follow the same path through step-auto-validate.

[Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/2-4-auto-decomposition-for-massive-repos.md#Previous Story Intelligence]

Stories 2.1→2.2→2.3→2.4 committed sequentially. Story 2.5 continues this pattern.

- **Commit onto**: This branch, next sequential commit after story 2.4
- **PR target**: `main` (single PR when Epic 2 is complete)

### Cross-Platform Requirements

Per project convention (PRs #311/#316/#366/#368/#387):
- Use `encoding="utf-8"` when reading/writing files in any Python code
- Use `Path.as_posix()` for paths in JSON output
- Windows CI (`windows-latest`) is the gatekeeper — Linux is silent on encoding issues

### Dependencies

| Dependency | Story | Status | What This Story Needs |
|-----------|-------|--------|----------------------|
| `step-auto-scope.md` | 2.1, 2.4 | done | The file being modified — docs-only detection inserted before §1 |
| `step-auto-brief.md` | 2.2 | done | The file being modified — docs-only conditional skip for §2/§3 |
| `skf-detect-docs.py` | 1.2 | done | NOT invoked for docs-only (user provides URL directly), but context for understanding what BS skips |
| `skf-write-skill-brief.py` | pre-existing | done | Canonical brief writer — already handles `source_type: docs-only` |
| `step-auto-validate.md` | 2.3 | done | Downstream consumer — validates and presents the brief for approval (informational, not modified) |

No blocking dependencies. All prerequisite step files and shared modules are complete.

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

1. **Do NOT invoke `skf-detect-docs.py` for docs-only URLs.** The script requires a GitHub repo URL for API calls. Passing a plain docs URL would fail or produce misleading results. The user-provided URL IS the doc source — no detection needed.

2. **Do NOT skip AN entirely for docs-only.** The pipeline alias `AN[auto] BS[auto] CS TS[min:90] EX` always starts with AN. AN must handle docs-only gracefully (short-circuit) rather than expecting the forger to skip it — that would require pipeline-level conditional logic that doesn't exist.

3. **Do NOT modify BS's interactive flow (gather-intent.md §3.2).** The existing interactive docs-only branch is complete and working. This story only modifies the `[auto]` mode path (step-auto-brief.md).

4. **Do NOT modify the forger's pipeline mode parsing.** The deepwiki alias expansion (`AN[auto] BS[auto] CS TS[min:90] EX`) is the same for both repo and docs-only URLs. URL type detection happens inside AN, not at the forger level.

5. **Do NOT create a separate pipeline alias for docs-only.** The epics specify a single `deepwiki` command that handles both repo URLs and doc URLs. The URL type heuristic in AN determines the path.

6. **Do NOT store doc content in the brief or analysis report.** Only the URL is stored. Content fetching is CS's responsibility (sub/fetch-docs.md handles it).

7. **Do NOT modify downstream workflows (CS, TS, EX).** They already handle `source_type: docs-only` correctly. This story's scope is AN detection + BS conditional skip.

content.content:
1	---
2	nextStepFile: 'health-check.md'
3	outputFile: '{forge_data_folder}/analyze-source-report-{project_name}.md'
4	shapeDetectScript: 'src/shared/scripts/skf-shape-detect.py'
5	---
6	
7	<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->
8	
9	# Step 1a: Auto-Scope Analysis
10	
11	## STEP GOAL:
12	
13	To automatically scope a repo using shape detection and export surface analysis, producing a scope and skill-brief.yaml without requiring manual input. This step replaces the interactive scan-project → identify-units → map-and-detect → recommend → generate-briefs chain when `{auto_mode}` is true.
14	
15	## Rules
16	
17	- Auto-proceed step — no user interaction required
18	- This step is conditional — only loaded when `[auto]` flag is present in the pipeline context
19	- Must produce the same output artifacts as the interactive chain: analysis report + skill-brief.yaml
20	- On unknown shape (exit code 1), fall back to `scan-project.md` (the normal interactive entry point)
21	- On error (exit code 2), HARD HALT with exit code 3 (`resolution-failure`)
22	
23	## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
24	
25	### 1. Load Context
26	
27	Read {outputFile} frontmatter to obtain:
28	- `project_paths[]` — the root(s) to analyze
29	- `forge_tier` — for brief generation
30	- `project_name`, `user_name`, `date`
31	
32	Load `references/step-shape-detect.md` as reference for shape detection invocation contract and shape→scope mapping.
33	
34	### 2. Manifest Scan
35	
36	Perform a lightweight manifest scan — find standard package manifests in the project root and workspace paths. Do NOT crawl the full directory tree.
37	
38	**For each path in `project_paths[]`:**
39	
40	1. Check the project root for: `package.json`, `pyproject.toml`, `Cargo.toml`
41	2. If a workspace configuration exists (e.g., `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, Cargo.toml `[workspace].members`), scan workspace member paths for additional manifests
42	3. Record each discovered manifest as `{path, type}` pairs
43	
44	**IF no manifests found:**
45	- Emit fallback message: "**Auto-scope could not find any package manifests — switching to interactive mode.**"
46	- Load, read fully, then execute `references/scan-project.md`. **STOP HERE.**
47	
48	### 3. Invoke Shape Detection
49	
50	Invoke the shape detection script with discovered manifests:
51	
52	```
53	uv run python {shapeDetectScript} --repo-url <project_path_or_url> --manifests <comma_separated_manifest_paths>
54	```
55	
56	Parse the JSON output: `{shape, signals, confidence, export_count, package_count}`
57	
58	**Handle exit codes:**
59	
60	- **Exit 0 (shape classified):** Continue to §3a.
61	- **Exit 1 (unknown shape):** Emit fallback message: "**Auto-scope could not classify this repo — switching to interactive mode.**" Load, read fully, then execute `references/scan-project.md`. **STOP HERE.**
62	- **Exit 2 (error):** HARD HALT with exit code 3 (`resolution-failure`). Emit the error envelope:
63	  ```
64	  SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"error","report_path":null,"brief_paths":[],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":0,"skipped":0,"maybe":0},"exit_code":3,"halt_reason":"resolution-failure","mode":"auto"}
65	  ```
66	
67	### 3a. Check Decomposition Thresholds
68	
69	Evaluate the shape detection output to determine whether this repo should be decomposed into multiple skills.
70	
71	**Threshold conditions** (both constants are `[PENDING VALIDATION]` — subject to empirical tuning):
72	
73	| Threshold | Condition | Signal |
74	|-----------|-----------|--------|
75	| Large export surface | `export_count > 500` `[PENDING VALIDATION]` | Single skill covering 500+ exports produces unwieldy output |
76	| Monorepo / multi-package | `package_count > 3` `[PENDING VALIDATION]` | 4+ packages are almost always multi-concern |
77	
78	**Decision:**
79	
80	- **Neither threshold met** → Continue to §4 (single-scope flow, entirely unchanged).
81	- **Either or both thresholds met** → Route to §4a (multi-scope decomposition flow). Log: "Auto-decomposition triggered: {reason} ({value} exceeds threshold {threshold} `[PENDING VALIDATION]`)" where reason is `export_threshold`, `package_threshold`, or `both`.
82	
83	### 4. Map Shape to Scope
84	
85	Apply the shape→scope.type mapping:
86	
87	| Shape (from skf-shape-detect.py) | scope.type | Condition |
88	|----------------------------------|------------|-----------|
89	| `library-API` | `full-library` | export_count ≤ 200 |
90	| `library-API` | `public-api` | export_count > 200 |
91	| `reference-app` | `reference-app` | — |
92	| `language-reference` | `full-library` | — |
93	| `stack-compose` | `full-library` | Multi-scope via §3a when `package_count > 3` `[PENDING VALIDATION]` |
94	
95	### 5. Generate Include/Exclude Patterns
96	
97	Generate `scope.include` and `scope.exclude` arrays from the detected language and project structure.
98	
99	**Detect primary language** from manifest type:
100	- `package.json` → TypeScript/JavaScript
101	- `pyproject.toml` → Python
102	- `Cargo.toml` → Rust
103	- `go.mod` → Go
104	
105	**Default patterns (adjust based on actual project structure):**
106	
107	| Language | Default include | Default exclude |
108	|----------|-----------------|-----------------|
109	| TypeScript/JavaScript | `['src/**/*.ts', 'src/**/*.tsx']` | `['**/*.test.ts', '**/*.spec.ts', '**/node_modules/**']` |
110	| Python | `['src/**/*.py']` or `['{package_name}/**/*.py']` | `['**/*_test.py', '**/test_*.py', '**/tests/**']` |
111	| Rust | `['src/**/*.rs']` | `['**/tests/**', '**/benches/**']` |
112	
113	**Adjust for actual layout:** If the project uses a non-standard layout (e.g., `lib/` instead of `src/`, or a named package directory for Python), detect and use the actual paths. Check for the existence of common source directories (`src/`, `lib/`, `pkg/`, the package name directory) and prefer the one that exists.
114	
115	### 6. Build Scope and Determine Skill Name
116	
117	Build the scope object:
118	```yaml
119	scope:
120	  type: '{mapped_scope_type}'
121	  include: ['{generated_include_patterns}']
122	  exclude: ['{generated_exclude_patterns}']
123	  notes: 'Auto-scoped from shape detection (shape: {shape}, confidence: {confidence})'
124	```
125	
126	Determine the skill name from the project name or package name (kebab-case, lowercase). Use the manifest `name` field if available, otherwise derive from the project directory name.
127	
128	Detect the primary language from the manifest ecosystem:
129	- `npm` → `typescript` (or `javascript` if no `.ts` files in includes)
130	- `python` → `python`
131	- `rust` → `rust`
132	
133	### 4a. Multi-Scope Decomposition
134	
135	This section is reached only from §3a when one or both decomposition thresholds are met. It replaces §4→§5→§6 for repos that will produce N > 1 skills.
136	
137	**Determine decomposition path:**
138	
139	- **Monorepo path** (`package_count > 3`): Use workspace package discovery from §2 manifest scan results. Each workspace package with its own manifest becomes a separate skill boundary. Name each skill as `{project_name}-{package_name}` (kebab-case). Trivial workspace members (no source files, no exports) are excluded.
140	- **Large-export path** (`export_count > 500`, single package): Group by top-level source directory modules (e.g., `src/auth/`, `src/core/`, `src/api/`). Each directory subtree with a meaningful export surface becomes a separate skill boundary. Candidate boundaries with fewer than ~50 exports `[PENDING VALIDATION]` should be merged into an "other" catch-all skill rather than becoming standalone skills. Name each skill as `{project_name}-{module_name}` (kebab-case). If no clear module structure exists (flat `src/` with all files at root level), **do not force decomposition** — fall back to single-scope flow at §4.
141	- **Combined path** (both thresholds met): Use the monorepo path. Package boundaries are explicit and take priority over export-count grouping (which is heuristic).
142	
143	**Per-boundary shape→scope mapping:**
144	
145	For each decomposed boundary, apply the shape→scope mapping from §4 independently — the boundary's local characteristics determine its scope.type:
146	
147	| Decomposition Type | Per-Boundary Shape Mapping |
148	|--------------------|---------------------------|
149	| Monorepo (`package_count > 3`) | Re-run the shape→scope heuristic ladder from `step-shape-detect.md` per package using each package's own manifest data. Packages may have different shapes (e.g., a `library-API` core + a `reference-app` CLI). |
150	| Large-export (`export_count > 500`) | All boundaries inherit the parent shape. scope.type varies by per-boundary export count (e.g., ≤200 → `full-library`, >200 → `public-api`). |
151	
152	### 5a. Generate Multi-Scope Patterns
153	
154	For each decomposed boundary, generate include/exclude patterns using the same language-aware rules as §5, but scoped to the boundary's source paths.
155	
156	- Monorepo boundaries: patterns are rooted at the package path (e.g., `packages/auth/src/**/*.ts` instead of `src/**/*.ts`)
157	- Large-export boundaries: patterns are rooted at the module directory (e.g., `src/auth/**/*.ts`)
158	
159	### 6a. Build Multi-Scope
160	
161	For each boundary, build a scope object following the same structure as §6.
162	
163	Include decomposition metadata in `scope.notes`: "Decomposed from {project_name} — boundary {i}/{N} ({reason})"
164	
165	Determine each boundary's skill name from the boundary-derived name (kebab-case, lowercase). Detect the primary language from each boundary's manifest ecosystem (same rules as §6).
166	
167	After building all N scopes, continue to §7 with the full set of boundaries.
168	
169	### 7. Write Analysis Report
170	
171	Update {outputFile} with auto-scope results.
172	
173	**Update frontmatter:**
174	```yaml
175	stepsCompleted: ['init', 'auto-scope']
176	lastStep: 'auto-scope'
177	confirmed_units:
178	  - name: '{skill_name}'
179	    shape: '{shape}'
180	    confidence: {confidence}
181	    export_count: {export_count}
182	    package_count: {package_count}
183	    boundary_path: '{boundary_path}'  # present only for decomposed units
184	  # ... N entries when decomposition is active
185	```
186	
187	**When decomposition was triggered (N > 1 units):**
188	
189	Add `decomposition` to frontmatter:
190	```yaml
191	decomposition:
192	  triggered: true
193	  reason: 'export_threshold' | 'package_threshold' | 'both'
194	  boundary_count: N
195	```
196	
197	Each `confirmed_units` entry includes `boundary_path` — the relative path to the boundary's root (e.g., `packages/core` for monorepo, `src/auth` for large-export). Omit the `decomposition` key entirely when single-scope (N = 1).
198	
199	**When single-scope (N = 1):** No `decomposition` key. `confirmed_units` contains a single entry (existing behavior).
200	
201	**Append body section:**
202	
203	For single-scope (unchanged):
204	```markdown
205	## Auto-Scope Analysis
206	
207	**Mode:** auto
208	**Shape:** {shape} (confidence: {confidence})
209	**Signals:** {signals list}
210	**Export Count:** {export_count}
211	**Package Count:** {package_count}
212	**Resolved Scope Type:** {scope_type}
213	**Include Patterns:** {include patterns}
214	**Exclude Patterns:** {exclude patterns}
215	```
216	
217	For multi-scope (N > 1):
218	```markdown
219	## Auto-Scope Analysis — Decomposition ({N} skills)
220	
221	**Mode:** auto
222	**Decomposition:** {reason} ({N} boundaries)
223	**Parent Shape:** {shape} (confidence: {confidence})
224	**Export Count:** {export_count}
225	**Package Count:** {package_count}
226	
227	### Boundary 1: {boundary_name}
228	**Scope Type:** {scope_type}
229	**Boundary Path:** {boundary_path}
230	**Include Patterns:** {include patterns}
231	**Exclude Patterns:** {exclude patterns}
232	**Rationale:** {boundary_rationale}
233	
234	### Boundary 2: {boundary_name}
235	...
236	```
237	
238	### 8. Write Skill Brief
239	
240	**For each confirmed unit** (1 for single-scope, N for decomposition):
241	
242	Create directory `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/` if it does not exist.
243	
244	Write `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/skill-brief.yaml` conforming to the skill-brief schema (`assets/skill-brief-schema.md`):
245	
246	```yaml
247	name: '{skill_name}'
248	version: '{detected_version or 1.0.0}'
249	source_repo: '{project_path}'
250	language: '{detected_language}'
251	scope:
252	  type: '{scope_type}'
253	  include:
254	    - '{include_patterns}'
255	  exclude:
256	    - '{exclude_patterns}'
257	  notes: 'Auto-scoped from shape detection (shape: {shape}, confidence: {confidence})'
258	description: '{1-3 sentence description based on shape, language, and manifest name}'
259	forge_tier: '{forge_tier}'
260	created: '{current_date}'
261	created_by: '{user_name}'
262	```
263	
264	**When decomposition is active (N > 1 units):**
265	
266	Loop over all N boundaries. For each boundary:
267	- `name` is the boundary-derived skill name (e.g., `my-monorepo-core`)
268	- `include`/`exclude` patterns are boundary-scoped (from §5a)
269	- `scope.notes` includes decomposition context: "Decomposed from {project_name} ({N} skills) — boundary {i}/{N}: {boundary_description}"
270	- `description` references the parent project and boundary role (e.g., "Core library package of the my-monorepo project, providing...")
271	- All N briefs share the same `version`, `source_repo`, `language`, `forge_tier`, `created`, `created_by` values as the parent project
272	
273	**Version detection:** Attempt to auto-detect the source version per the version detection rules in `assets/skill-brief-schema.md`. Fall back to `1.0.0` if detection fails.
274	
275	### 9. Emit Result Envelope
276	
277	Emit the `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON` envelope on stdout:
278	
279	```
280	SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success","report_path":"{outputFile_path}","brief_paths":["{brief_path_1}","{brief_path_2}",...,"{brief_path_N}"],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":N,"skipped":0,"maybe":0},"exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto"}
281	```
282	
283	`brief_paths` contains N paths (one per confirmed unit). `unit_counts.confirmed` is N. The envelope JSON format is structurally unchanged — `brief_paths` was already an array and `unit_counts.confirmed` was already a number. No breaking change for downstream consumers.
284	
285	### 10. Write Result Contract
286	
287	Write the result contract per `shared/references/output-contract-schema.md`: the per-run record at `{forge_data_folder}/analyze-source-result-{YYYYMMDD-HHmmss}.json` (UTC timestamp, resolution to seconds) and a copy at `{forge_data_folder}/analyze-source-result-latest.json`. `outputs` lists all N brief paths and `summary` includes the brief count N.
288	
289	If `{onCompleteCommand}` is non-empty, invoke it now with `--result-path={result_json_path}`.
290	
291	### 11. Chain to Health Check
292	
293	Load, read fully, then execute {nextStepFile} to run the shared workflow health check.
294	
295	## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
296	
297	ONLY WHEN the analysis report has been updated, the skill-brief.yaml written and validated, the result envelope emitted, and the result contract saved will you load and read fully {nextStepFile} to begin the health check.
298

file.content:
---
nextStepFile: 'health-check.md'
outputFile: '{forge_data_folder}/analyze-source-report-{project_name}.md'
shapeDetectScript: 'src/shared/scripts/skf-shape-detect.py'
---

<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->

# Step 1a: Auto-Scope Analysis

## STEP GOAL:

To automatically scope a repo using shape detection and export surface analysis, producing a scope and skill-brief.yaml without requiring manual input. This step replaces the interactive scan-project → identify-units → map-and-detect → recommend → generate-briefs chain when `{auto_mode}` is true.

## Rules

- Auto-proceed step — no user interaction required
- This step is conditional — only loaded when `[auto]` flag is present in the pipeline context
- Must produce the same output artifacts as the interactive chain: analysis report + skill-brief.yaml
- On unknown shape (exit code 1), fall back to `scan-project.md` (the normal interactive entry point)
- On error (exit code 2), HARD HALT with exit code 3 (`resolution-failure`)

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

### 1. Load Context

Read {outputFile} frontmatter to obtain:
- `project_paths[]` — the root(s) to analyze
- `forge_tier` — for brief generation
- `project_name`, `user_name`, `date`

Load `references/step-shape-detect.md` as reference for shape detection invocation contract and shape→scope mapping.

### 2. Manifest Scan

Perform a lightweight manifest scan — find standard package manifests in the project root and workspace paths. Do NOT crawl the full directory tree.

**For each path in `project_paths[]`:**

1. Check the project root for: `package.json`, `pyproject.toml`, `Cargo.toml`
2. If a workspace configuration exists (e.g., `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, Cargo.toml `[workspace].members`), scan workspace member paths for additional manifests
3. Record each discovered manifest as `{path, type}` pairs

**IF no manifests found:**
- Emit fallback message: "**Auto-scope could not find any package manifests — switching to interactive mode.**"
- Load, read fully, then execute `references/scan-project.md`. **STOP HERE.**

### 3. Invoke Shape Detection

Invoke the shape detection script with discovered manifests:

```
uv run python {shapeDetectScript} --repo-url <project_path_or_url> --manifests <comma_separated_manifest_paths>
```

Parse the JSON output: `{shape, signals, confidence, export_count, package_count}`

**Handle exit codes:**

- **Exit 0 (shape classified):** Continue to §3a.
- **Exit 1 (unknown shape):** Emit fallback message: "**Auto-scope could not classify this repo — switching to interactive mode.**" Load, read fully, then execute `references/scan-project.md`. **STOP HERE.**
- **Exit 2 (error):** HARD HALT with exit code 3 (`resolution-failure`). Emit the error envelope:
  ```
  SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"error","report_path":null,"brief_paths":[],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":0,"skipped":0,"maybe":0},"exit_code":3,"halt_reason":"resolution-failure","mode":"auto"}
  ```

### 3a. Check Decomposition Thresholds

Evaluate the shape detection output to determine whether this repo should be decomposed into multiple skills.

**Threshold conditions** (both constants are `[PENDING VALIDATION]` — subject to empirical tuning):

| Threshold | Condition | Signal |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| Large export surface | `export_count > 500` `[PENDING VALIDATION]` | Single skill covering 500+ exports produces unwieldy output |
| Monorepo / multi-package | `package_count > 3` `[PENDING VALIDATION]` | 4+ packages are almost always multi-concern |

**Decision:**

- **Neither threshold met** → Continue to §4 (single-scope flow, entirely unchanged).
- **Either or both thresholds met** → Route to §4a (multi-scope decomposition flow). Log: "Auto-decomposition triggered: {reason} ({value} exceeds threshold {threshold} `[PENDING VALIDATION]`)" where reason is `export_threshold`, `package_threshold`, or `both`.

### 4. Map Shape to Scope

Apply the shape→scope.type mapping:

| Shape (from skf-shape-detect.py) | scope.type | Condition |
|----------------------------------|------------|-----------|
| `library-API` | `full-library` | export_count ≤ 200 |
| `library-API` | `public-api` | export_count > 200 |
| `reference-app` | `reference-app` | — |
| `language-reference` | `full-library` | — |
| `stack-compose` | `full-library` | Multi-scope via §3a when `package_count > 3` `[PENDING VALIDATION]` |

### 5. Generate Include/Exclude Patterns

Generate `scope.include` and `scope.exclude` arrays from the detected language and project structure.

**Detect primary language** from manifest type:
- `package.json` → TypeScript/JavaScript
- `pyproject.toml` → Python
- `Cargo.toml` → Rust
- `go.mod` → Go

**Default patterns (adjust based on actual project structure):**

| Language | Default include | Default exclude |
|----------|-----------------|-----------------|
| TypeScript/JavaScript | `['src/**/*.ts', 'src/**/*.tsx']` | `['**/*.test.ts', '**/*.spec.ts', '**/node_modules/**']` |
| Python | `['src/**/*.py']` or `['{package_name}/**/*.py']` | `['**/*_test.py', '**/test_*.py', '**/tests/**']` |
| Rust | `['src/**/*.rs']` | `['**/tests/**', '**/benches/**']` |

**Adjust for actual layout:** If the project uses a non-standard layout (e.g., `lib/` instead of `src/`, or a named package directory for Python), detect and use the actual paths. Check for the existence of common source directories (`src/`, `lib/`, `pkg/`, the package name directory) and prefer the one that exists.

### 6. Build Scope and Determine Skill Name

Build the scope object:
```yaml
scope:
  type: '{mapped_scope_type}'
  include: ['{generated_include_patterns}']
  exclude: ['{generated_exclude_patterns}']
  notes: 'Auto-scoped from shape detection (shape: {shape}, confidence: {confidence})'
```

Determine the skill name from the project name or package name (kebab-case, lowercase). Use the manifest `name` field if available, otherwise derive from the project directory name.

Detect the primary language from the manifest ecosystem:
- `npm` → `typescript` (or `javascript` if no `.ts` files in includes)
- `python` → `python`
- `rust` → `rust`

### 4a. Multi-Scope Decomposition

This section is reached only from §3a when one or both decomposition thresholds are met. It replaces §4→§5→§6 for repos that will produce N > 1 skills.

**Determine decomposition path:**

- **Monorepo path** (`package_count > 3`): Use workspace package discovery from §2 manifest scan results. Each workspace package with its own manifest becomes a separate skill boundary. Name each skill as `{project_name}-{package_name}` (kebab-case). Trivial workspace members (no source files, no exports) are excluded.
- **Large-export path** (`export_count > 500`, single package): Group by top-level source directory modules (e.g., `src/auth/`, `src/core/`, `src/api/`). Each directory subtree with a meaningful export surface becomes a separate skill boundary. Candidate boundaries with fewer than ~50 exports `[PENDING VALIDATION]` should be merged into an "other" catch-all skill rather than becoming standalone skills. Name each skill as `{project_name}-{module_name}` (kebab-case). If no clear module structure exists (flat `src/` with all files at root level), **do not force decomposition** — fall back to single-scope flow at §4.
- **Combined path** (both thresholds met): Use the monorepo path. Package boundaries are explicit and take priority over export-count grouping (which is heuristic).

**Per-boundary shape→scope mapping:**

For each decomposed boundary, apply the shape→scope mapping from §4 independently — the boundary's local characteristics determine its scope.type:

| Decomposition Type | Per-Boundary Shape Mapping |
|--------------------|---------------------------|
| Monorepo (`package_count > 3`) | Re-run the shape→scope heuristic ladder from `step-shape-detect.md` per package using each package's own manifest data. Packages may have different shapes (e.g., a `library-API` core + a `reference-app` CLI). |
| Large-export (`export_count > 500`) | All boundaries inherit the parent shape. scope.type varies by per-boundary export count (e.g., ≤200 → `full-library`, >200 → `public-api`). |

### 5a. Generate Multi-Scope Patterns

For each decomposed boundary, generate include/exclude patterns using the same language-aware rules as §5, but scoped to the boundary's source paths.

- Monorepo boundaries: patterns are rooted at the package path (e.g., `packages/auth/src/**/*.ts` instead of `src/**/*.ts`)
- Large-export boundaries: patterns are rooted at the module directory (e.g., `src/auth/**/*.ts`)

### 6a. Build Multi-Scope

For each boundary, build a scope object following the same structure as §6.

Include decomposition metadata in `scope.notes`: "Decomposed from {project_name} — boundary {i}/{N} ({reason})"

Determine each boundary's skill name from the boundary-derived name (kebab-case, lowercase). Detect the primary language from each boundary's manifest ecosystem (same rules as §6).

After building all N scopes, continue to §7 with the full set of boundaries.

### 7. Write Analysis Report

Update {outputFile} with auto-scope results.

**Update frontmatter:**
```yaml
stepsCompleted: ['init', 'auto-scope']
lastStep: 'auto-scope'
confirmed_units:
  - name: '{skill_name}'
    shape: '{shape}'
    confidence: {confidence}
    export_count: {export_count}
    package_count: {package_count}
    boundary_path: '{boundary_path}'  # present only for decomposed units
  # ... N entries when decomposition is active
```

**When decomposition was triggered (N > 1 units):**

Add `decomposition` to frontmatter:
```yaml
decomposition:
  triggered: true
  reason: 'export_threshold' | 'package_threshold' | 'both'
  boundary_count: N
```

Each `confirmed_units` entry includes `boundary_path` — the relative path to the boundary's root (e.g., `packages/core` for monorepo, `src/auth` for large-export). Omit the `decomposition` key entirely when single-scope (N = 1).

**When single-scope (N = 1):** No `decomposition` key. `confirmed_units` contains a single entry (existing behavior).

**Append body section:**

For single-scope (unchanged):
```markdown
## Auto-Scope Analysis

**Mode:** auto
**Shape:** {shape} (confidence: {confidence})
**Signals:** {signals list}
**Export Count:** {export_count}
**Package Count:** {package_count}
**Resolved Scope Type:** {scope_type}
**Include Patterns:** {include patterns}
**Exclude Patterns:** {exclude patterns}
```

For multi-scope (N > 1):
```markdown
## Auto-Scope Analysis — Decomposition ({N} skills)

**Mode:** auto
**Decomposition:** {reason} ({N} boundaries)
**Parent Shape:** {shape} (confidence: {confidence})
**Export Count:** {export_count}
**Package Count:** {package_count}

### Boundary 1: {boundary_name}
**Scope Type:** {scope_type}
**Boundary Path:** {boundary_path}
**Include Patterns:** {include patterns}
**Exclude Patterns:** {exclude patterns}
**Rationale:** {boundary_rationale}

### Boundary 2: {boundary_name}
...
```

### 8. Write Skill Brief

**For each confirmed unit** (1 for single-scope, N for decomposition):

Create directory `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/` if it does not exist.

Write `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/skill-brief.yaml` conforming to the skill-brief schema (`assets/skill-brief-schema.md`):

```yaml
name: '{skill_name}'
version: '{detected_version or 1.0.0}'
source_repo: '{project_path}'
language: '{detected_language}'
scope:
  type: '{scope_type}'
  include:
    - '{include_patterns}'
  exclude:
    - '{exclude_patterns}'
  notes: 'Auto-scoped from shape detection (shape: {shape}, confidence: {confidence})'
description: '{1-3 sentence description based on shape, language, and manifest name}'
forge_tier: '{forge_tier}'
created: '{current_date}'
created_by: '{user_name}'
```

**When decomposition is active (N > 1 units):**

Loop over all N boundaries. For each boundary:
- `name` is the boundary-derived skill name (e.g., `my-monorepo-core`)
- `include`/`exclude` patterns are boundary-scoped (from §5a)
- `scope.notes` includes decomposition context: "Decomposed from {project_name} ({N} skills) — boundary {i}/{N}: {boundary_description}"
- `description` references the parent project and boundary role (e.g., "Core library package of the my-monorepo project, providing...")
- All N briefs share the same `version`, `source_repo`, `language`, `forge_tier`, `created`, `created_by` values as the parent project

**Version detection:** Attempt to auto-detect the source version per the version detection rules in `assets/skill-brief-schema.md`. Fall back to `1.0.0` if detection fails.

### 9. Emit Result Envelope

Emit the `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON` envelope on stdout:

```
SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success","report_path":"{outputFile_path}","brief_paths":["{brief_path_1}","{brief_path_2}",...,"{brief_path_N}"],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":N,"skipped":0,"maybe":0},"exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto"}
```

`brief_paths` contains N paths (one per confirmed unit). `unit_counts.confirmed` is N. The envelope JSON format is structurally unchanged — `brief_paths` was already an array and `unit_counts.confirmed` was already a number. No breaking change for downstream consumers.

### 10. Write Result Contract

Write the result contract per `shared/references/output-contract-schema.md`: the per-run record at `{forge_data_folder}/analyze-source-result-{YYYYMMDD-HHmmss}.json` (UTC timestamp, resolution to seconds) and a copy at `{forge_data_folder}/analyze-source-result-latest.json`. `outputs` lists all N brief paths and `summary` includes the brief count N.

If `{onCompleteCommand}` is non-empty, invoke it now with `--result-path={result_json_path}`.

### 11. Chain to Health Check

Load, read fully, then execute {nextStepFile} to run the shared workflow health check.

## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE

ONLY WHEN the analysis report has been updated, the skill-brief.yaml written and validated, the result envelope emitted, and the result contract saved will you load and read fully {nextStepFile} to begin the health check.

content.content:
1	---
2	nextStepFile: 'step-auto-validate.md'
3	validateBriefSchemaProbeOrder:
4	  - '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/shared/scripts/skf-validate-brief-schema.py'
5	  - '{project-root}/src/shared/scripts/skf-validate-brief-schema.py'
6	writeSkillBriefProbeOrder:
7	  - '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/shared/scripts/skf-write-skill-brief.py'
8	  - '{project-root}/src/shared/scripts/skf-write-skill-brief.py'
9	emitBriefEnvelopeProbeOrder:
10	  - '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/shared/scripts/skf-emit-brief-result-envelope.py'
11	  - '{project-root}/src/shared/scripts/skf-emit-brief-result-envelope.py'
12	detectDocsScript: 'src/shared/scripts/skf-detect-docs.py'
13	---
14	
15	<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->
16	
17	# Step 1a: Auto-Brief Generation
18	
19	## STEP GOAL:
20	
21	To enrich an upstream skill brief (produced by AN auto-scope) with documentation URLs discovered via `skf-detect-docs.py`, validate the enriched brief, and write it through the canonical writer. Envelope emission is deferred to step-auto-validate.md, which presents the brief for user approval before continuing. This step replaces the interactive gather-intent → analyze-target → scope-definition → confirm-brief → write-brief chain when `[auto]` mode is active.
22	
23	## Rules
24	
25	- Auto-proceed step — no user interaction required
26	- This step is conditional — only loaded when `[auto]` flag is present in the pipeline context
27	- Must produce the same output artifact as the interactive chain: a validated `skill-brief.yaml`
28	- Doc detection is best-effort — failures do not halt the pipeline
29	- Do NOT re-derive scope fields from the upstream brief — AN already set them correctly
30	- Do NOT render YAML or JSON envelopes in the LLM — delegate to deterministic scripts
31	
32	## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
33	
34	### 1. Load Upstream Brief
35	
36	Read the upstream brief path from `{brief_path}` (passed by the forger from AN's `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON` `brief_paths[]`).
37	
38	**IF `{brief_path}` is not set or the file does not exist:**
39	- HARD HALT with exit code 2 (`input-missing`): "**Auto-brief requires an upstream brief — `brief_path` is missing or the file does not exist at `{brief_path}`.**"
40	- Emit error envelope per §6 with `halt_reason: "input-missing"`.
41	
42	**Resolve `{validateBriefSchemaHelper}`** from `{validateBriefSchemaProbeOrder}`; first existing path wins. HALT if no candidate exists.
43	
44	Validate the upstream brief against the schema:
45	
46	```bash
47	uv run {validateBriefSchemaHelper} {brief_path}
48	```
49	
50	The script returns JSON `{valid, errors[], warnings[], halt_reason, brief}`.
51	
52	- **`valid: false`** — the upstream brief is malformed. HARD HALT with exit code 2 (`input-invalid`): "**Upstream brief at `{brief_path}` is invalid: {first error message}.**" Emit error envelope per §6 with `halt_reason: "input-invalid"`.
53	- **`valid: true`** — proceed with the parsed `brief` payload. Surface any non-empty `warnings[]` to the log.
54	
55	Extract from the parsed brief:
56	- `skill_name` ← `brief.name`
57	- `version` ← `brief.version`
58	- `source_repo` ← `brief.source_repo`
59	- `language` ← `brief.language`
60	- `scope_type` ← `brief.scope.type`
61	- `forge_tier` ← `brief.forge_tier`
62	- `description` ← `brief.description`
63	- `created` ← `brief.created`
64	- `created_by` ← `brief.created_by`
65	- All scope fields: `scope.include`, `scope.exclude`, `scope.notes`, `scope.rationale`, `scope.amendments`, `scope.tier_a_include`
66	- Optional fields: `source_type`, `source_authority`, `doc_urls`, `target_version`, `target_ref`, `source_ref`, `scripts_intent`, `assets_intent`
67	
68	### 2. Run Doc Detection
69	
70	Invoke doc detection to discover documentation URLs for the source repo:
71	
72	```bash
73	uv run python {project-root}/{detectDocsScript} --repo-url {source_repo}
74	```
75	
76	`--repo-url` is always required (the script uses it for GitHub API calls). If a local clone is also available at `{local_clone_path}`, add `--local-path {local_clone_path}` to enable docs-folder scanning in addition to API-based detection.
77	
78	**Handle exit codes:**
79	
80	- **Exit 0 (found docs):** Parse the JSON output array. Each entry has `{url, detected_via, content_hash, content_type}`. Proceed to §3 with the detected docs.
81	- **Exit 1 (none found):** Log: "No external documentation found — brief generated from source analysis only." Proceed to §4 with no doc enrichment.
82	- **Exit 2 (error):** Log warning: "Doc detection failed — proceeding without doc enrichment." Do NOT halt — doc enrichment is best-effort. Proceed to §4 with no doc enrichment.
83	
84	### 3. Enrich Brief with Detected Docs
85	
86	For each detected doc entry, create a brief `doc_urls` entry:
87	
88	- `url` ← `url` (direct copy)
89	- `label` ← derive from `content_type` if available:
90	  - `"api-docs"` → `"API Documentation"`
91	  - `"guide"` → `"Guide"`
92	  - `"reference"` → `"Reference"`
93	  - Otherwise derive from `detected_via`:
94	    - `"homepageUrl"` → `"Homepage"`
95	    - `"readme_link"` → `"README Link"`
96	    - `"pages_api"` → `"GitHub Pages"`
97	    - `"docs_folder"` → `"Docs Folder"`
98	
99	If the upstream brief already has `doc_urls`, merge the detected docs with the existing entries (deduplicate by URL — existing entries take precedence).
100	
101	### 4. Validate Enriched Brief
102	
103	Assemble the enriched brief context as a flat JSON object following the write-brief §3 contract:
104	
105	```json
106	{
107	  "name":             "{skill_name}",
108	  "target_version":   "{target_version or null}",
109	  "detected_version": null,
110	  "source_type":      "{source_type or 'source'}",
111	  "source_repo":      "{source_repo}",
112	  "language":         "{language}",
113	  "description":      "{description}",
114	  "forge_tier":       "{forge_tier}",
115	  "created":          "{created}",
116	  "created_by":       "{created_by}",
117	  "scope_type":       "{scope_type}",
118	  "scope_include":    ["{scope.include patterns}"],
119	  "scope_exclude":    ["{scope.exclude patterns}"],
120	  "scope_notes":      "{scope.notes or ''}",
121	  "scope_rationale":  null,
122	  "scope_tier_a_include": null,
123	  "scope_amendments":     null,
124	  "doc_urls":         [{"url": "...", "label": "..."}],
125	  "scripts_intent":   "{scripts_intent or null}",
126	  "assets_intent":    "{assets_intent or null}",
127	  "source_authority": "{source_authority or null}",
128	  "target_ref":       "{target_ref or null}",
129	  "source_ref":       "{source_ref or null}",
130	  "version_resolved": "{version}"
131	}
132	```
133	
134	The `version_resolved` key pins the output to the upstream brief's version — without it, the writer's precedence logic falls through to `1.0.0` since `target_version` and `detected_version` are both null on the auto path.
135	
136	### 5. Write Enriched Brief
137	
138	**Resolve `{writeSkillBriefHelper}`** from `{writeSkillBriefProbeOrder}`; first existing path wins. HALT if no candidate exists.
139	
140	Write the enriched brief through the canonical writer:
141	
142	```bash
143	echo '<context-json>' | uv run {writeSkillBriefHelper} write --target {forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/skill-brief.yaml --from-flat
144	```
145	
146	**On script failure (non-zero exit):**
147	- Exit 1 (validation/invariant): Emit error envelope per §6 with `halt_reason: "input-invalid"`, then HARD HALT.
148	- Exit 2 (I/O failure): Emit error envelope per §6 with `halt_reason: "write-failed"`, then HARD HALT.
149	
150	**On success:** Capture `brief_path` and `version` from the response envelope for step-auto-validate's envelope emission.
151	
152	### 6. Error Envelope (Canonical)
153	
154	Every HARD HALT in this step emits the error envelope on stderr:
155	
156	**Resolve `{emitBriefEnvelopeHelper}`** from `{emitBriefEnvelopeProbeOrder}`; first existing path wins. HALT if no candidate exists.
157	
158	```bash
159	echo '{"status":"error","skill_name":"{skill_name or unknown}","halt_reason":"{reason}","mode":"auto"}' | \
160	  uv run {emitBriefEnvelopeHelper} emit --target stderr
161	```
162	
163	### 7. Chain to Auto-Validate
164	
165	Load, read fully, then execute {nextStepFile} to present the auto-brief validation gate, where the user can approve, edit, or reject the brief before the pipeline continues.
166	
167	## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
168	
169	ONLY WHEN the enriched brief has been written and validated will you load and read fully {nextStepFile} to begin the auto-brief validation gate.
170
